description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer tasked with enforcing **policy-as-code** rules against a web-server access log that already exists on the machine.

    1. Location of the raw log you must analyse  
       • /home/user/logs/webserver/access.log

    2. Policies to enforce  
       P-1  Detect every request that returned an HTTP status in the **5xx** range.  
       P-2  Detect every request whose *requested path* begins with **/admin** (e.g. “/admin”, “/admin/settings”, “/admin/foo/bar”, …).  
       Each log line that satisfies at least one policy is considered a **violation**.

    3. Artefacts you must create under /home/user/reports (create the directory if it does not exist)

       a) CSV report “/home/user/reports/security_violation_report.csv” with **exactly this header**  
          timestamp,client_ip,request_method,request_path,status_code,violation_type  
          • timestamp           → the RFC-3164 time-stamp inside the square brackets but **without** the brackets  
          • client_ip           → first field in the log line  
          • request_method      → GET/POST/… between the first quotes  
          • request_path        → path component between the first quotes (no protocol / host)  
          • status_code         → integer 3-digit HTTP status  
          • violation_type      → either “5xx_error” or “admin_access_attempt”  
          One CSV data row per violating request; no extra columns, no quoting, one newline per row.

       b) JSON summary “/home/user/reports/summary.json” with exactly four top-level keys:  
          {  
            "total_requests": <int>,  
            "total_violations": <int>,  
            "5xx_errors": <int>,  
            "admin_access_attempts": <int>  
          }  
          All values must be integers.

       c) Audit trail “/home/user/reports/policy_audit.log” – append a single line containing the word “COMPLETED” (all caps). No other content is required.

    4. System-state expectations
       • Do **not** modify the original /home/user/logs/webserver/access.log.  
       • Place the three artefacts exactly as named and in the directory specified.  
       • Directory /home/user/reports must end up with mode 755 (rwxr-xr-x) so the test harness can read the files.

    5. Complexity constraint  
       Limit yourself to a sensible sequence of 5-10 terminal commands in total.

    When you finish, the three files must exist and contain data in the precise formats described so an automated test suite can parse them verbatim.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth for automated grading.
        The log file already exists before the agent runs.
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <!-- directory layout -->
        <dir path="/home/user/logs" mode="755"/>
        <dir path="/home/user/logs/webserver" mode="755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/webserver/access.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
192.168.0.10 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:15:01 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 612
54.23.12.1 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:15:05 +0000] "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 200 1420
203.0.113.55 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:16:13 +0000] "GET /admin HTTP/1.1" 403 721
198.51.100.23 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:17:42 +0000] "GET /images/logo.png HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
192.0.2.44 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:18:09 +0000] "GET /admin/settings HTTP/1.1" 404 512
203.0.113.55 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:18:57 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 500 890
54.23.12.1 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:19:31 +0000] "GET /api/data HTTP/1.1" 503 112
198.51.100.23 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:20:15 +0000] "GET /contact.html HTTP/1.1" 200 430
192.0.2.44 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:20:45 +0000] "GET /admin HTTP/1.1" 401 512
203.0.113.55 - - [10/Jan/2023:08:21:10 +0000] "POST /api/upload HTTP/1.1" 201 1340
        ]]></file>

        <!-- reports directory is present but empty -->
        <dir path="/home/user/reports" mode="755"/>
    </initial_state>

    <final_state>
        <!-- required outputs for verification -->
        <file path="/home/user/reports/security_violation_report.csv" mode="644"><![CDATA[
timestamp,client_ip,request_method,request_path,status_code,violation_type
10/Jan/2023:08:16:13 +0000,203.0.113.55,GET,/admin,403,admin_access_attempt
10/Jan/2023:08:18:09 +0000,192.0.2.44,GET,/admin/settings,404,admin_access_attempt
10/Jan/2023:08:18:57 +0000,203.0.113.55,GET,/dashboard,500,5xx_error
10/Jan/2023:08:19:31 +0000,54.23.12.1,GET,/api/data,503,5xx_error
10/Jan/2023:08:20:45 +0000,192.0.2.44,GET,/admin,401,admin_access_attempt
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/reports/summary.json" mode="644"><![CDATA[
{
  "total_requests": 10,
  "total_violations": 5,
  "5xx_errors": 2,
  "admin_access_attempts": 3
}
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/reports/policy_audit.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[COMPLETED
]]></file>
    </final_state>
